creator: ralf.folkerts@gmx.de
text:
After Updating my System several weeks ago I got no sound any longer. I then posted this problem in freebsd-stable on 05/13/2012 but didn't get a reply. So I sent a mail to mav@ on 05/28/2012 asking if this is a know problem (after the Rewrite it was stated that sound may be broken with newer ATI cards IIRC). Didn't get a response either.

On 06/09/2012 I cvsupped RELENG_9 again and again did a buildworld/buildkernel/installworld/installkernel. Still no sound. I then reverted the files that were modified with rev. 230130 to their rev. before rev. 232798 (this was the MFC). Did a buildkernel/installkernel -- and got sound again (well, not through PA but that might be another Problem; ogg123 played OK).

I'd mostly like to know if this is the intended behaviour, if my config is bad or if this is a bug. And if it's a bug if there is a path to get it fixed.

I'll just paste my post to freebsd-stable below; hope it contains all Info required.

MTIA!
Cheers,
_ralf_


Hi,

on 2012-05-05 I csupped and updated my FreeBSD 9-STABLE System (which was running 9-STABLE checked out 2012-01-12). After the Update I did not get any sound, however. Neither from CL (mpg123, ogg123) nor from GUI (KDE). I didn't find anything on this list (which I'm subscribed to), but when checking the "current" List I found there was a rewrite of snd_hda which probably was MFCed (and which, probably!, causes my Problem). The MB is an Asus M4A87TD/USB3.

To make sure it's not a H/W thing I csupped to RELENG_9 dated 2012-01-12 again, made kernel and world, mergemastered the configs - and got sound.

Sound-related in /etc/loader.conf
sound_load="YES"
snd_hda_load="YES"

Sound-related in my /etc/sysctl.conf (I use the analog Output of the Card)
dev.pcm.0.play.vchans=4
hw.snd.default_unit=1

Would be cool to get this fixed, as I'm currently running the soundless RELENG_9.

MTIA!
